_doc: Committee-regret sampler training run per §12.15 diagnostic. Tests whether window-level dilution is the rate ceiling bottleneck. 50% uniform + 50% regret-weighted windows from pre-scored pool. Keeps KD mechanism minimal (low alpha, no RRDSD) to ISOLATE the data-side effect.
_expected_behavior_if_hypothesis_correct: eval slope jumps from -0.007/100 to <=-0.015/100 OR train-eval gap shrinks >=0.01 BPB by step 400

_notes.mechanism: Plain classical KL-KD at alpha=0.03 (matches iter5 baseline). NO RRDSD, NO multi-teacher, NO CKA, NO TAID, NO UG. The ONE variable under test is: does 50/50 regret-weighted window sampling break the -0.007/100 eval slope ceiling?
_notes.prerequisite: Run code/probe_committee_regret.py FIRST to produce results/committee_regret_scores.npz (needs GPU, ~5-10 min).
_notes.success_test: If step 400 eval BPB <= 1.385 (delta vs iter5 seed: -0.013, over 400 steps = -0.033/100 effective rate), window-selection thesis is CONFIRMED and becomes new mainline.
_notes.failure_test: If step 400 eval >= 1.400 (no faster than r1b), window-selection is disproven — pivot to long-horizon/structural objectives per §12.15 Phase C table.
_notes.kill_rules_rationale: Standard iter5-era kill thresholds (not tighter than RRDSD) — this is a DATA test not a mechanism test, so give it room.
